title: Military Aircraft
description: Designing and fielding a fleet of technologically superior military aircraft is often an imperative for national and regional security, but the cost involved in acquiring and maintaining such fleets places a significant burden on defense budgets and can impact a nation's ability to project force. RAND research has provided policymakers with essential evaluations and recommendations to implement cost savings in the design, acquisition, and fielding of military aircraft.
text:
Improving Interoperability with Allies and Partners: Aircraft Maintenance and Base Operating Support
The U.S. Air Force must generate and sustain airpower from dispersed, partner-owned locations. This brief discusses interoperability not as an end state but as a spectrum of cooperation that is tailored to requirements, standards, and capabilities.
Russia's Military Procurement Is a Warning for Europe
Russia's naval and missile buildup has been informed by its invasion of Ukraine, but the risk extends further. Russia's industrial investments are building a coercive toolkit that explicitly targets European territory.
2025 RAND Wrap-Up: Research That Defined Our Year
RAND's mission is clear: We tackle hard problems and tough questions, helping policymakers make informed, robust decisions amid risk and uncertainty. Here are some ways RAND supported decisionmakers in 2025.
In Europe, the Problem Is Deterrence, Not Drones
If NATO nations allow themselves to be distracted by a few drones flying from Russia, they risk missing the bigger picture when it comes to both Russia and drones. To truly protect itself, Europe must restore its ability and resolve to strike back, not just defend.
Natalie Crawford, Longtime RAND Analyst Who Guided Research on Modernizing Air Force, Dies at 86
Natalie Crawford, a RAND Corporation analyst and leading authority on U.S. air and space power who worked closely with top Air Force leaders to shape a research agenda focused on the effectiveness and modernization of the service, has died. She was 86 and had worked at RAND for more than 60 years before retiring earlier this year.
Improving Partner Interoperability for U.S. Air Forces in Europe: Aircraft Maintenance and Cross-Servicing
U.S. Air Forces in Europe is pursuing greater interoperability with its European partners. The authors review policy and interview experts, finding both progress and persistent obstacles on F-35 and F-16 efforts.
The People's Liberation Army's Approach to Manned-Unmanned Teaming: Theory and Practice
This analysis of publicly available information on Chinese perspectives of manned-unmanned teaming (MUM-T) in air combat can inform U.S. Department of the Air Force leaders and strategists as they develop advanced U.S. MUM-T warfighting concepts.
Responding to President Trump's Recent Executive Orders on Drones
A recent executive order aims to make the United States a leader in drone technology, balancing rapid unmanned aerial system growth and innovation with the need to address security threats and protect critical infrastructure.
Assessing Progress on Air Base Defense: Past Investments and Future Options
The growing missile threat in the Pacific region has led the U.S. Air Force (USAF) to assess air base defense. RAND researchers examined how the USAF has invested in air base defense and how it can prioritize air base defense moving forward.
Defending U.S. Military Bases Against Drones? A Recent Tabletop Exercise Explores How
Drones increasingly threaten military personnel and bases, both at home and abroad. In March, a tabletop exercise with over 100 participants from more than 24 federal agencies explored counter-drone operations to defend U.S. military bases.
